pairs.question: When and where was Amy Spencer born?
pairs.answer: Amy Spencer was born on 19 September 1985 in Wigan, Greater Manchester, England.

pairs.question: What notable achievements did Amy Spencer accomplish as a teenager in athletics?
pairs.answer: As a teenager, Amy Spencer won the British U13 best for 80m in 1998 and the AAA U15 200m title in 1999. She was also voted BBC Young Sports Personality of the Year in 2001.

pairs.question: What medals did Amy Spencer win at the 2002 World Junior Championships?
pairs.answer: At the 2002 World Junior Championships, Amy Spencer won a bronze medal in the 4 x 100 metres relay and a silver medal in the 4 x 400 metres relay.

pairs.question: What were Amy Spencer's personal best times in the 100m and 200m events?
pairs.answer: Amy Spencer's personal best times were 11.66 seconds in the 100 metres (achieved in June 2001 in Mannheim) and 23.20 seconds in the 200 metres (indoor track, March 2003 in Birmingham). Her outdoor personal best in the 200m was 23.45 seconds.

pairs.question: In which year did Amy Spencer retire from athletics, and which club did she represent?
pairs.answer: Amy Spencer retired from athletics in 2005. She ran with the Belgrave Harriers club during her career.

pairs.question: How does Amy Spencer's athletic career compare to her brother Tom Spencer's career?
pairs.answer: Amy Spencer was a retired English sprinter known for her achievements in the 200 metres, while her younger brother Tom Spencer is a professional rugby league footballer who made his senior debut for Wigan Warriors in the Super League in 2012.
